title: How proprietary formats have become Microsoft’s main tool for lock-in
excerpt: In the previous article, we explored the importance of standards: how the unspoken agreements governing electrical sockets, paper sizes and file formats form the foundations of a world in which choices remain open and power is not concentrated in the hands of a single player. We concluded with a question: if open standards are so beneficial, why aren’t they universally adopted? The answer, in the case of document formats, lies in a single page produced by Microsoft Office. Getting rid of it is harder than it seems. For decades, the dominant format for office documents has been that produced by Microsoft Office. content_html:
In the previous article, we explored the importance of standards: how the unspoken agreements governing electrical sockets, paper sizes and file formats form the foundations of a world in which choices remain open and power is not concentrated in the hands of a single player. We concluded with a question: if open standards are so beneficial, why aren’t they universally adopted?

The answer, in the case of document formats, lies in a single page produced by Microsoft Office. Getting rid of it is harder than it seems.

A file is never just a file

When you save a document on your computer, you are choosing a format — that is, the language in which your document is written in a way that the computer can understand: the set of rules that determines how words, tables, images and formatting instructions are stored and, consequently, how they can be retrieved, shared and read in the future.

For decades, the dominant format for office documents has been that produced by Microsoft Office. Initially as binary files with extensions such as DOC and XLS, then as XML-based formats introduced with Office 2007: DOCX, XLSX and PPTX. These formats are used by hundreds of millions of people. They are the lingua franca of offices, schools, public administrations and courts around the world.

Furthermore, in significant and decisive ways, they are proprietary — meaning they belong to Microsoft, are controlled by Microsoft and serve Microsoft’s interests in ways that may not align with the interests of users.

Understanding how all this works — and why it matters far beyond mere matters of software preference — is the aim of this article.

The architecture of dependency

Proprietary formats create dependency through a mechanism that is simple in principle and extraordinarily effective in practice: they make the data contained in a document inseparable from the software used to create it.

This is not a law of physics, but a design choice.

An open format — a format whose specifications are published, freely available and implementable by any software without restrictions — stores information in such a way that any compliant application can read, write and reproduce it faithfully.

A proprietary format, by contrast, may contain undocumented features, private extensions or behaviours that only the original software implements correctly. The document may be opened by other applications, but it cannot always be reproduced faithfully.

The practical consequence is familiar to anyone who has tried to open a Microsoft Office document in another application: the formatting becomes distorted, bullet points shift, tables lose their proportions, and headings appear different.

A presentation that looked polished in PowerPoint seems slapdash in a different viewer: the content is all there, whilst the document, strictly speaking, is not.

This is “lock-in”: it is not a padlock, it is not a technical ban, it is not a contractual restriction, but a silent and persistent friction that makes any work outside the Microsoft ecosystem seem slightly off, slightly unreliable, slightly unprofessional — and ensures that the easiest route is to return to the tools that produce documents with the expected appearance.

The standard that isn’t a standard

Microsoft formats have been submitted to international standardisation bodies and approved. This has been used, time and again, to argue that concerns about “lock-in” are exaggerated — that OOXML, the Office Open XML format, is as open a standard as any other, and that the playing field is level.

The reality is considerably more complicated.

The standardisation of OOXML was one of the most contested processes in the history of ISO: national standardisation bodies reported procedural irregularities, and the votes were contested. The process has left an indelible mark on the credibility of international standards, and has resulted in a specification of extraordinary length and complexity — running to thousands of pages — which did not describe a format designed for interoperability, but rather the existing behaviour of Microsoft Office, including legacy behaviours, undocumented features and implementation details specific to Microsoft’s source code.

No other software could fully implement the format, yet it was required to do so out of respect for its users, who needed to exchange documents with Microsoft users.

The version of OOXML that was standardised — OOXML Transitional — initially co-existed with a stricter variant, OOXML Strict, which eliminated most of the problematic legacy elements, but not all. Moreover, Microsoft Office has always used OOXML Transitional as the default format, and has relegated OOXML Strict to the bottom of the options (to prevent it from being used).

The practical effect is that the format used daily by hundreds of millions of people is the one that only Microsoft’s own software implements correctly, whilst the cleaner variant — which other software could actually support — is not used, and has now even disappeared from some versions.

A standard that only one implementation fully supports is, from a functional point of view, a proprietary format with a standardisation certificate.

Lock-in, from the individual to the institution

Dependence on document formats is the main mechanism of lock-in, but it is not the only one. We discussed the layering of dependencies at length in a previous article, so we will not revisit the subject here. Levels of dependence vary depending on the importance of the documents involved and the size of the organisation producing them.

For an individual user, a document with altered layout is simply an inconvenience. For a law firm, it may mean that a contract submitted to court does not match the version in the client’s file. For a hospital, it may mean that a clinical form is printed incorrectly. For a government department, it may mean that a document appears differently depending on the software used: a silent and unintended form of unequal access to public information.

At the level of public administration, this dependency takes on a dimension that goes beyond operational efficiency. A government that archives official documents in a format controlled by a private company has, strictly speaking, delegated the custody of its institutional memory to that company. Today, documents are readable because Microsoft continues to support the format, but whether they can be read in twenty years’ time will depend on the company’s decisions, for reasons that have nothing to do with the public interest.

This is not a hypothetical risk: formats are phased out, software versions change, and features present in one version of Office may behave differently — or not work at all — in another.

The history of digital documents is littered with files that cannot be opened because the software with which they were created no longer exists or no longer works on modern systems. Proprietary formats accelerate this risk by concentrating the knowledge needed to interpret them within an organisation whose commercial interests may, at any time, diverge from the interests of those who depend on access to their own documents.

What true sovereignty requires

A truly independent document — one that displays identically on any system, in any country, for any user, regardless of the software used — requires informed choices at every stage of its creation.

At the format level, it requires an open standard such as the Open Document Format (ODF), whose specifications are published, freely implementable and managed by a body independent of any single vendor. ODF is an international ISO standard that has undergone a legitimate standardisation process and whose specifications can be fully implemented by any software that chooses to do so. LibreOffice, the leading open-source office suite, uses ODF natively. The same should apply to any other self-respecting open-source office application.

In terms of fonts, it requires open fonts, the designs of which are published under licences that allow any software to implement them and any user to install them without cost or restrictions. Open font repositories offer a wide range of high-quality options that have no proprietary dependencies.

In terms of templates and workflows, it requires institutional policies that specify open formats and open fonts as the default for all official documents — not as an aesthetic preference, but as a governance requirement, in the same way that a public administration might specify accessibility standards or data protection requirements.

At the archiving level, this requires a commitment to formats specifically designed for long-term preservation — such as PDF/A for documents intended for permanent archiving — whose specifications are public and whose readability does not depend on the commercial decisions of a single supplier.

None of this is technically complex, but it requires a conscious decision by someone with the authority to do so.

A document is never innocent

A document is an argument made visible. It is also, always, a set of dependencies made invisible.

When an institution sends a letter formatted with a proprietary font, embedded in a proprietary format, produced by proprietary software, it is not communicating information but perpetuating a dependency — in workflows, in the expectations of correspondents, in staff memory and in the implicit message to recipients — that this is precisely how documents work, that there are no alternatives, that the infrastructure of written communication belongs to someone else, and that it has always been this way.

Digital sovereignty begins with the recognition that this is a choice: the file format is a choice, the font on the page is a choice, and the software is a choice. And choices, unlike facts, can vary.

That document, which continues to be regarded as a “seemingly” innocent piece of paper, is in reality no longer a piece of paper — and hasn’t been for some time — but an executable file that is interpreted by software, and as such is no longer innocent but, in many cases, the insidious tool of lock-in.

Microsoft Office Icons: Ms Office Icon Vectors by Vecteezy
Protest against OOXML: Techrights
Innocent piece of paper: The Mazloom Law Firm, LLC

blocks.html_en: In the previous article, we explored the importance of standards: how the unspoken agreements governing electrical sockets, paper sizes and file formats form the foundations of a world in which choices remain open and power is not concentrated in the hands of a single player. We concluded with a question: if open standards are so beneficial, why aren’t they universally adopted?

sentences.en: In the previous article, we explored the importance of standards: how the unspoken agreements governing electrical sockets, paper sizes and file formats form the foundations of a world in which choices remain open and power is not concentrated in the hands of a single player.
sentences.zh: 在上一篇文章中，我们探讨了标准的重要性：管理电源插座、纸张尺寸和文件格式的不言而喻的协议如何构成一个世界的基础，在这个世界中，选择保持开放，权力不集中在单个玩家手中。

sentences.en: We concluded with a question: if open standards are so beneficial, why aren’t they universally adopted?
sentences.zh: 我们最后提出一个问题：如果开放标准如此有益，为什么它们没有得到普遍采用？

blocks.html_en: The answer, in the case of document formats, lies in a single page produced by Microsoft Office. Getting rid of it is harder than it seems.

sentences.en: The answer, in the case of document formats, lies in a single page produced by Microsoft Office.
sentences.zh: 就文档格式而言，答案在于 Microsoft Office 生成的单个页面。

sentences.en: Getting rid of it is harder than it seems.
sentences.zh: 摆脱它比看起来更难。

blocks.html_en: A file is never just a file

sentences.en: A file is never just a file
sentences.zh: 文件永远不仅仅是一个文件

blocks.html_en: When you save a document on your computer, you are choosing a format — that is, the language in which your document is written in a way that the computer can understand: the set of rules that determines how words, tables, images and formatting instructions are stored and, consequently, how they can be retrieved, shared and read in the future.

sentences.en: When you save a document on your computer, you are choosing a format — that is, the language in which your document is written in a way that the computer can understand: the set of rules that determines how words, tables, images and formatting instructions are stored and, consequently, how they can be retrieved, shared and read in the future.
sentences.zh: 当您在计算机上保存文档时，您正在选择一种格式，即以计算机可以理解的方式编写文档的语言：确定如何存储文字、表格、图像和格式指令以及将来如何检索、共享和阅读它们的一组规则。

blocks.html_en: For decades, the dominant format for office documents has been that produced by Microsoft Office. Initially as binary files with extensions such as DOC and XLS, then as XML-based formats introduced with Office 2007: DOCX, XLSX and PPTX. These formats are used by hundreds of millions of people. They are the lingua franca of offices, schools, public administrations and courts around the world.

sentences.en: For decades, the dominant format for office documents has been that produced by Microsoft Office.
sentences.zh: 几十年来，Office 文档的主导格式一直是 Microsoft Office 生成的格式。

sentences.en: Initially as binary files with extensions such as DOC and XLS, then as XML-based formats introduced with Office 2007: DOCX, XLSX and PPTX.
sentences.zh: 最初是带有 DOC 和 XLS 等扩展名的二进制文件，然后是 Office 2007 中引入的基于 XML 的格式：DOCX、XLSX 和 PPTX。

sentences.en: These formats are used by hundreds of millions of people.
sentences.zh: 这些格式被数亿人使用。

sentences.en: They are the lingua franca of offices, schools, public administrations and courts around the world.
sentences.zh: 它们是世界各地办公室、学校、公共行政部门和法院的通用语言。

blocks.html_en: Furthermore, in significant and decisive ways, they are proprietary — meaning they belong to Microsoft, are controlled by Microsoft and serve Microsoft’s interests in ways that may not align with the interests of users.

sentences.en: Furthermore, in significant and decisive ways, they are proprietary — meaning they belong to Microsoft, are controlled by Microsoft and serve Microsoft’s interests in ways that may not align with the interests of users.
sentences.zh: 此外，在重要且决定性的方面，它们是专有的——这意味着它们属于微软，由微软控制，并以可能与用户利益不一致的方式服务于微软的利益。

blocks.html_en: Understanding how all this works — and why it matters far beyond mere matters of software preference — is the aim of this article.

sentences.en: Understanding how all this works — and why it matters far beyond mere matters of software preference — is the aim of this article.
sentences.zh: 了解这一切是如何工作的——以及为什么它的重要性远远超出了单纯的软件偏好问题——是本文的目的。

blocks.html_en: The architecture of dependency

sentences.en: The architecture of dependency
sentences.zh: 依赖架构

blocks.html_en: Proprietary formats create dependency through a mechanism that is simple in principle and extraordinarily effective in practice: they make the data contained in a document inseparable from the software used to create it.

sentences.en: Proprietary formats create dependency through a mechanism that is simple in principle and extraordinarily effective in practice: they make the data contained in a document inseparable from the software used to create it.
sentences.zh: 专有格式通过一种原则上简单但在实践中非常有效的机制创建依赖性：它们使文档中包含的数据与用于创建它的软件密不可分。

blocks.html_en: This is not a law of physics, but a design choice.

sentences.en: This is not a law of physics, but a design choice.
sentences.zh: 这不是物理定律，而是设计选择。

blocks.html_en: An open format — a format whose specifications are published, freely available and implementable by any software without restrictions — stores information in such a way that any compliant application can read, write and reproduce it faithfully.

sentences.en: An open format — a format whose specifications are published, freely available and implementable by any software without restrictions — stores information in such a way that any compliant application can read, write and reproduce it faithfully.
sentences.zh: 开放格式——其规范已发布、可免费使用并可由任何软件不受限制地实现的格式——以任何兼容应用程序都可以忠实地读取、写入和复制信息的方式存储信息。

blocks.html_en: A proprietary format, by contrast, may contain undocumented features, private extensions or behaviours that only the original software implements correctly. The document may be opened by other applications, but it cannot always be reproduced faithfully.

sentences.en: A proprietary format, by contrast, may contain undocumented features, private extensions or behaviours that only the original software implements correctly.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，专有格式可能包含只有原始软件才能正确实现的未记录的功能、私有扩展或行为。

sentences.en: The document may be opened by other applications, but it cannot always be reproduced faithfully.
sentences.zh: 该文档可以由其他应用程序打开，但并不总是能够忠实地再现。

blocks.html_en: The practical consequence is familiar to anyone who has tried to open a Microsoft Office document in another application: the formatting becomes distorted, bullet points shift, tables lose their proportions, and headings appear different.

sentences.en: The practical consequence is familiar to anyone who has tried to open a Microsoft Office document in another application: the formatting becomes distorted, bullet points shift, tables lose their proportions, and headings appear different.
sentences.zh: 对于任何尝试在其他应用程序中打开 Microsoft Office 文档的人来说，实际后果都是熟悉的：格式变得扭曲，项目符号点发生变化，表格失去比例，标题看起来不同。

blocks.html_en: A presentation that looked polished in PowerPoint seems slapdash in a different viewer: the content is all there, whilst the document, strictly speaking, is not.

sentences.en: A presentation that looked polished in PowerPoint seems slapdash in a different viewer: the content is all there, whilst the document, strictly speaking, is not.
sentences.zh: 在 PowerPoint 中看起来精美的演示文稿在不同的观看者中似乎显得草率：内容都在那里，而文档，严格来说，却并非如此。

blocks.html_en: This is “lock-in”: it is not a padlock, it is not a technical ban, it is not a contractual restriction, but a silent and persistent friction that makes any work outside the Microsoft ecosystem seem slightly off, slightly unreliable, slightly unprofessional — and ensures that the easiest route is to return to the tools that produce documents with the expected appearance.

sentences.en: This is “lock-in”: it is not a padlock, it is not a technical ban, it is not a contractual restriction, but a silent and persistent friction that makes any work outside the Microsoft ecosystem seem slightly off, slightly unreliable, slightly unprofessional — and ensures that the easiest route is to return to the tools that produce documents with the expected appearance.
sentences.zh: 这就是“锁定”：它不是挂锁，不是技术禁令，也不是合同限制，而是一种无声而持续的摩擦，使微软生态系统之外的任何工作都显得有点偏离、有点不可靠、有点不专业——并确保最简单的途径是返回到生成具有预期外观的文档的工具。

blocks.html_en: The standard that isn’t a standard

sentences.en: The standard that isn’t a standard
sentences.zh: 不是标准的标准

blocks.html_en: Microsoft formats have been submitted to international standardisation bodies and approved. This has been used, time and again, to argue that concerns about “lock-in” are exaggerated — that OOXML, the Office Open XML format, is as open a standard as any other, and that the playing field is level.

sentences.en: Microsoft formats have been submitted to international standardisation bodies and approved.
sentences.zh: Microsoft 格式已提交给国际标准化机构并获得批准。

sentences.en: This has been used, time and again, to argue that concerns about “lock-in” are exaggerated — that OOXML, the Office Open XML format, is as open a standard as any other, and that the playing field is level.
sentences.zh: 这已经被一次又一次地用来证明对“锁定”的担忧被夸大了——OOXML（Office Open XML 格式）与任何其他标准一样开放，而且竞争环境是公平的。

blocks.html_en: The reality is considerably more complicated.

sentences.en: The reality is considerably more complicated.
sentences.zh: 现实要复杂得多。

blocks.html_en: The standardisation of OOXML was one of the most contested processes in the history of ISO: national standardisation bodies reported procedural irregularities, and the votes were contested. The process has left an indelible mark on the credibility of international standards, and has resulted in a specification of extraordinary length and complexity — running to thousands of pages — which did not describe a format designed for interoperability, but rather the existing behaviour of Microsoft Office, including legacy behaviours, undocumented features and implementation details specific to Microsoft’s source code.

sentences.en: The standardisation of OOXML was one of the most contested processes in the history of ISO: national standardisation bodies reported procedural irregularities, and the votes were contested.
sentences.zh: OOXML 的标准化是 ISO 历史上最有争议的过程之一：国家标准化机构报告了程序上的违规行为，并且投票也存在争议。

sentences.en: The process has left an indelible mark on the credibility of international standards, and has resulted in a specification of extraordinary length and complexity — running to thousands of pages — which did not describe a format designed for interoperability, but rather the existing behaviour of Microsoft Office, including legacy behaviours, undocumented features and implementation details specific to Microsoft’s source code.
sentences.zh: 这一过程在国际标准的可信度上留下了不可磨灭的印记，并产生了一份极其长度和复杂的规范（长达数千页），它没有描述为互操作性而设计的格式，而是描述了 Microsoft Office 的现有行为，包括遗留行为、未记录的功能和特定于 Microsoft 源代码的实现细节。

blocks.html_en: No other software could fully implement the format, yet it was required to do so out of respect for its users, who needed to exchange documents with Microsoft users.

sentences.en: No other software could fully implement the format, yet it was required to do so out of respect for its users, who needed to exchange documents with Microsoft users.
sentences.zh: 没有其他软件可以完全实现该格式，但出于对用户的尊重，它必须这样做，因为用户需要与 Microsoft 用户交换文档。

blocks.html_en: The version of OOXML that was standardised — OOXML Transitional — initially co-existed with a stricter variant, OOXML Strict, which eliminated most of the problematic legacy elements, but not all. Moreover, Microsoft Office has always used OOXML Transitional as the default format, and has relegated OOXML Strict to the bottom of the options (to prevent it from being used).

sentences.en: The version of OOXML that was standardised — OOXML Transitional — initially co-existed with a stricter variant, OOXML Strict, which eliminated most of the problematic legacy elements, but not all.
sentences.zh: 标准化的 OOXML 版本（OOXML Transitional）最初与更严格的变体 OOXML Strict 共存，后者消除了大部分有问题的遗留元素，但不是全部。

sentences.en: Moreover, Microsoft Office has always used OOXML Transitional as the default format, and has relegated OOXML Strict to the bottom of the options (to prevent it from being used).
sentences.zh: 此外，Microsoft Office 始终使用 OOXML Transitional 作为默认格式，并将 OOXML Strict 降级到选项的底部（以防止使用它）。

blocks.html_en: The practical effect is that the format used daily by hundreds of millions of people is the one that only Microsoft’s own software implements correctly, whilst the cleaner variant — which other software could actually support — is not used, and has now even disappeared from some versions.

sentences.en: The practical effect is that the format used daily by hundreds of millions of people is the one that only Microsoft’s own software implements correctly, whilst the cleaner variant — which other software could actually support — is not used, and has now even disappeared from some versions.
sentences.zh: 实际效果是，数亿人每天使用的格式只有微软自己的软件才能正确实现，而其他软件实际上可以支持的更清晰的变体则没有被使用，现在甚至已经从某些版本中消失了。

blocks.html_en: A standard that only one implementation fully supports is, from a functional point of view, a proprietary format with a standardisation certificate.

sentences.en: A standard that only one implementation fully supports is, from a functional point of view, a proprietary format with a standardisation certificate.
sentences.zh: 从功能的角度来看，只有一个实现完全支持的标准是具有标准化证书的专有格式。

blocks.html_en: Lock-in, from the individual to the institution

sentences.en: Lock-in, from the individual to the institution
sentences.zh: 锁定，从个人到机构

blocks.html_en: Dependence on document formats is the main mechanism of lock-in, but it is not the only one. We discussed the layering of dependencies at length in a previous article, so we will not revisit the subject here. Levels of dependence vary depending on the importance of the documents involved and the size of the organisation producing them.

sentences.en: Dependence on document formats is the main mechanism of lock-in, but it is not the only one.
sentences.zh: 对文档格式的依赖是锁定的主要机制，但不是唯一的机制。

sentences.en: We discussed the layering of dependencies at length in a previous article, so we will not revisit the subject here.
sentences.zh: 我们在上一篇文章中详细讨论了依赖关系的分层，因此我们不会在这里重新讨论这个主题。

sentences.en: Levels of dependence vary depending on the importance of the documents involved and the size of the organisation producing them.
sentences.zh: 依赖程度取决于所涉及文档的重要性以及生成这些文档的组织的规模。

blocks.html_en: For an individual user, a document with altered layout is simply an inconvenience. For a law firm, it may mean that a contract submitted to court does not match the version in the client’s file. For a hospital, it may mean that a clinical form is printed incorrectly. For a government department, it may mean that a document appears differently depending on the software used: a silent and unintended form of unequal access to public information.

sentences.en: For an individual user, a document with altered layout is simply an inconvenience.
sentences.zh: 对于个人用户来说，布局改变的文档只会带来不便。

sentences.en: For a law firm, it may mean that a contract submitted to court does not match the version in the client’s file.
sentences.zh: 对于律师事务所来说，这可能意味着提交给法院的合同与客户文件中的版本不符。

sentences.en: For a hospital, it may mean that a clinical form is printed incorrectly.
sentences.zh: 对于医院来说，这可能意味着临床表格打印不正确。

sentences.en: For a government department, it may mean that a document appears differently depending on the software used: a silent and unintended form of unequal access to public information.
sentences.zh: 对于政府部门来说，这可能意味着根据所使用的软件，文档的显示方式有所不同：一种无声的、无意的形式，对公共信息的访问不平等。

blocks.html_en: At the level of public administration, this dependency takes on a dimension that goes beyond operational efficiency. A government that archives official documents in a format controlled by a private company has, strictly speaking, delegated the custody of its institutional memory to that company. Today, documents are readable because Microsoft continues to support the format, but whether they can be read in twenty years’ time will depend on the company’s decisions, for reasons that have nothing to do with the public interest.

sentences.en: At the level of public administration, this dependency takes on a dimension that goes beyond operational efficiency.
sentences.zh: 在公共行政层面，这种依赖性超出了运营效率的范围。

sentences.en: A government that archives official documents in a format controlled by a private company has, strictly speaking, delegated the custody of its institutional memory to that company.
sentences.zh: 严格来说，以私营公司控制的格式归档官方文件的政府已将其机构记忆的保管权委托给了该公司。

sentences.en: Today, documents are readable because Microsoft continues to support the format, but whether they can be read in twenty years’ time will depend on the company’s decisions, for reasons that have nothing to do with the public interest.
sentences.zh: 今天，文档是可读的，因为微软继续支持该格式，但二十年后是否可以阅读它们将取决于公司的决定，其原因与公共利益无关。

blocks.html_en: This is not a hypothetical risk: formats are phased out, software versions change, and features present in one version of Office may behave differently — or not work at all — in another.

sentences.en: This is not a hypothetical risk: formats are phased out, software versions change, and features present in one version of Office may behave differently — or not work at all — in another.
sentences.zh: 这不是假设的风险：格式被淘汰，软件版本发生变化，一个版本的 Office 中提供的功能在另一个版本中可能表现不同，或者根本不起作用。

blocks.html_en: The history of digital documents is littered with files that cannot be opened because the software with which they were created no longer exists or no longer works on modern systems. Proprietary formats accelerate this risk by concentrating the knowledge needed to interpret them within an organisation whose commercial interests may, at any time, diverge from the interests of those who depend on access to their own documents.

sentences.en: The history of digital documents is littered with files that cannot be opened because the software with which they were created no longer exists or no longer works on modern systems.
sentences.zh: 数字文档的历史中充斥着无法打开的文件，因为创建它们的软件已不存在或不再适用于现代系统。

sentences.en: Proprietary formats accelerate this risk by concentrating the knowledge needed to interpret them within an organisation whose commercial interests may, at any time, diverge from the interests of those who depend on access to their own documents.
sentences.zh: 专有格式将解释它们所需的知识集中在一个组织内，而该组织的商业利益可能随时与那些依赖于访问自己文档的人的利益产生分歧，从而加速了这种风险。

blocks.html_en: What true sovereignty requires

sentences.en: What true sovereignty requires
sentences.zh: 真正的主权需要什么

blocks.html_en: A truly independent document — one that displays identically on any system, in any country, for any user, regardless of the software used — requires informed choices at every stage of its creation.

sentences.en: A truly independent document — one that displays identically on any system, in any country, for any user, regardless of the software used — requires informed choices at every stage of its creation.
sentences.zh: 一份真正独立的文档——无论使用什么软件，在任何国家、任何系统上对任何用户都显示相同的文档——需要在其创建的每个阶段做出明智的选择。

blocks.html_en: At the format level, it requires an open standard such as the Open Document Format (ODF), whose specifications are published, freely implementable and managed by a body independent of any single vendor. ODF is an international ISO standard that has undergone a legitimate standardisation process and whose specifications can be fully implemented by any software that chooses to do so. LibreOffice, the leading open-source office suite, uses ODF natively. The same should apply to any other self-respecting open-source office application.

sentences.en: At the format level, it requires an open standard such as the Open Document Format (ODF), whose specifications are published, freely implementable and managed by a body independent of any single vendor.
sentences.zh: 在格式层面，它需要一个开放标准，例如开放文档格式（ODF），其规范由独立于任何单一供应商的机构发布、自由实施和管理。

sentences.en: ODF is an international ISO standard that has undergone a legitimate standardisation process and whose specifications can be fully implemented by any software that chooses to do so.
sentences.zh: ODF 是一项国际 ISO 标准，经过了合法的标准化流程，其规范可以由任何选择这样做的软件完全实施。

sentences.en: LibreOffice, the leading open-source office suite, uses ODF natively.
sentences.zh: LibreOffice 是领先的开源办公套件，本身就使用 ODF。

sentences.en: The same should apply to any other self-respecting open-source office application.
sentences.zh: 这同样适用于任何其他有自尊的开源办公应用程序。

blocks.html_en: In terms of fonts, it requires open fonts, the designs of which are published under licences that allow any software to implement them and any user to install them without cost or restrictions. Open font repositories offer a wide range of high-quality options that have no proprietary dependencies.

sentences.en: In terms of fonts, it requires open fonts, the designs of which are published under licences that allow any software to implement them and any user to install them without cost or restrictions.
sentences.zh: 在字体方面，它需要开放字体，其设计是在许可下发布的，允许任何软件实现它们，并且任何用户可以无成本或无限制地安装它们。

sentences.en: Open font repositories offer a wide range of high-quality options that have no proprietary dependencies.
sentences.zh: 开放字体存储库提供了各种没有专有依赖性的高质量选项。

blocks.html_en: In terms of templates and workflows, it requires institutional policies that specify open formats and open fonts as the default for all official documents — not as an aesthetic preference, but as a governance requirement, in the same way that a public administration might specify accessibility standards or data protection requirements.

sentences.en: In terms of templates and workflows, it requires institutional policies that specify open formats and open fonts as the default for all official documents — not as an aesthetic preference, but as a governance requirement, in the same way that a public administration might specify accessibility standards or data protection requirements.
sentences.zh: 在模板和工作流程方面，它需要将开放格式和开放字体指定为所有官方文档的默认设置的制度政策——不是作为审美偏好，而是作为治理要求，就像公共行政部门可能指定可访问性标准或数据保护要求一样。

blocks.html_en: At the archiving level, this requires a commitment to formats specifically designed for long-term preservation — such as PDF/A for documents intended for permanent archiving — whose specifications are public and whose readability does not depend on the commercial decisions of a single supplier.

sentences.en: At the archiving level, this requires a commitment to formats specifically designed for long-term preservation — such as PDF/A for documents intended for permanent archiving — whose specifications are public and whose readability does not depend on the commercial decisions of a single supplier.
sentences.zh: 在归档层面，这需要致力于专门为长期保存而设计的格式（例如用于永久归档的文档的 PDF/A），其规范是公开的，其可读性不取决于单个供应商的商业决策。

blocks.html_en: None of this is technically complex, but it requires a conscious decision by someone with the authority to do so.

sentences.en: None of this is technically complex, but it requires a conscious decision by someone with the authority to do so.
sentences.zh: 这些在技术上并不复杂，但需要有权这样做的人有意识地做出决定。

blocks.html_en: A document is never innocent

sentences.en: A document is never innocent
sentences.zh: 文件从来都不是无辜的

blocks.html_en: A document is an argument made visible. It is also, always, a set of dependencies made invisible.

sentences.en: A document is an argument made visible.
sentences.zh: 文档是可见的论证。

sentences.en: It is also, always, a set of dependencies made invisible.
sentences.zh: 它也始终是一组不可见的依赖关系。

blocks.html_en: When an institution sends a letter formatted with a proprietary font, embedded in a proprietary format, produced by proprietary software, it is not communicating information but perpetuating a dependency — in workflows, in the expectations of correspondents, in staff memory and in the implicit message to recipients — that this is precisely how documents work, that there are no alternatives, that the infrastructure of written communication belongs to someone else, and that it has always been this way.

sentences.en: When an institution sends a letter formatted with a proprietary font, embedded in a proprietary format, produced by proprietary software, it is not communicating information but perpetuating a dependency — in workflows, in the expectations of correspondents, in staff memory and in the implicit message to recipients — that this is precisely how documents work, that there are no alternatives, that the infrastructure of written communication belongs to someone else, and that it has always been this way.
sentences.zh: 当一个机构发送一封采用专有字体、嵌入专有格式、由专有软件生成的信件时，它不是在传达信息，而是在工作流程中、在通讯员的期望中、在工作人员的记忆中以及在给收件人的隐含信息中永久存在一种依赖性，即这正是文档的工作方式，没有其他选择，书面通信的基础设施属于其他人，而且一直都是这样。

blocks.html_en: Digital sovereignty begins with the recognition that this is a choice: the file format is a choice, the font on the page is a choice, and the software is a choice. And choices, unlike facts, can vary.

sentences.en: Digital sovereignty begins with the recognition that this is a choice: the file format is a choice, the font on the page is a choice, and the software is a choice.
sentences.zh: 数字主权首先要认识到这是一种选择：文件格式是一种选择，页面上的字体是一种选择，软件也是一种选择。

sentences.en: And choices, unlike facts, can vary.
sentences.zh: 与事实不同，选择可能会有所不同。

blocks.html_en: That document, which continues to be regarded as a “seemingly” innocent piece of paper, is in reality no longer a piece of paper — and hasn’t been for some time — but an executable file that is interpreted by software, and as such is no longer innocent but, in many cases, the insidious tool of lock-in.

sentences.en: That document, which continues to be regarded as a “seemingly” innocent piece of paper, is in reality no longer a piece of paper — and hasn’t been for some time — but an executable file that is interpreted by software, and as such is no longer innocent but, in many cases, the insidious tool of lock-in.
sentences.zh: 该文件仍然被认为是一张“看似”无辜的文件，实际上不再是一张纸——而且已经有一段时间了——而是一个由软件解释的可执行文件，因此不再是无辜的，而是在许多情况下成为阴险的锁定工具。

blocks.html_en:
Microsoft Office Icons: Ms Office Icon Vectors by Vecteezy
Protest against OOXML: Techrights
Innocent piece of paper: The Mazloom Law Firm, LLC

sentences.en: Microsoft Office Icons: Ms Office Icon Vectors by Vecteezy Protest against OOXML: Techrights Innocent piece of paper: The Mazloom Law Firm, LLC
sentences.zh: Microsoft Office 图标：Vecteezy 制作的 Ms Office 图标矢量 抗议 OOXML：Techrights 无辜的纸片：The Mazloom Law Firm, LLC

content_html_zh:
在上一篇文章中，我们探讨了标准的重要性：管理电源插座、纸张尺寸和文件格式的不言而喻的协议如何构成一个世界的基础，在这个世界中，选择保持开放，权力不集中在单个玩家手中。 我们最后提出一个问题：如果开放标准如此有益，为什么它们没有得到普遍采用？

就文档格式而言，答案在于 Microsoft Office 生成的单个页面。 摆脱它比看起来更难。

文件永远不仅仅是一个文件

当您在计算机上保存文档时，您正在选择一种格式，即以计算机可以理解的方式编写文档的语言：确定如何存储文字、表格、图像和格式指令以及将来如何检索、共享和阅读它们的一组规则。

几十年来，Office 文档的主导格式一直是 Microsoft Office 生成的格式。 最初是带有 DOC 和 XLS 等扩展名的二进制文件，然后是 Office 2007 中引入的基于 XML 的格式：DOCX、XLSX 和 PPTX。 这些格式被数亿人使用。 它们是世界各地办公室、学校、公共行政部门和法院的通用语言。

此外，在重要且决定性的方面，它们是专有的——这意味着它们属于微软，由微软控制，并以可能与用户利益不一致的方式服务于微软的利益。

了解这一切是如何工作的——以及为什么它的重要性远远超出了单纯的软件偏好问题——是本文的目的。

依赖架构

专有格式通过一种原则上简单但在实践中非常有效的机制创建依赖性：它们使文档中包含的数据与用于创建它的软件密不可分。

这不是物理定律，而是设计选择。

开放格式——其规范已发布、可免费使用并可由任何软件不受限制地实现的格式——以任何兼容应用程序都可以忠实地读取、写入和复制信息的方式存储信息。

相比之下，专有格式可能包含只有原始软件才能正确实现的未记录的功能、私有扩展或行为。 该文档可以由其他应用程序打开，但并不总是能够忠实地再现。

对于任何尝试在其他应用程序中打开 Microsoft Office 文档的人来说，实际后果都是熟悉的：格式变得扭曲，项目符号点发生变化，表格失去比例，标题看起来不同。

在 PowerPoint 中看起来精美的演示文稿在不同的观看者中似乎显得草率：内容都在那里，而文档，严格来说，却并非如此。

这就是“锁定”：它不是挂锁，不是技术禁令，也不是合同限制，而是一种无声而持续的摩擦，使微软生态系统之外的任何工作都显得有点偏离、有点不可靠、有点不专业——并确保最简单的途径是返回到生成具有预期外观的文档的工具。

不是标准的标准

Microsoft 格式已提交给国际标准化机构并获得批准。 这已经被一次又一次地用来证明对“锁定”的担忧被夸大了——OOXML（Office Open XML 格式）与任何其他标准一样开放，而且竞争环境是公平的。

现实要复杂得多。

OOXML 的标准化是 ISO 历史上最有争议的过程之一：国家标准化机构报告了程序上的违规行为，并且投票也存在争议。 这一过程在国际标准的可信度上留下了不可磨灭的印记，并产生了一份极其长度和复杂的规范（长达数千页），它没有描述为互操作性而设计的格式，而是描述了 Microsoft Office 的现有行为，包括遗留行为、未记录的功能和特定于 Microsoft 源代码的实现细节。

没有其他软件可以完全实现该格式，但出于对用户的尊重，它必须这样做，因为用户需要与 Microsoft 用户交换文档。

标准化的 OOXML 版本（OOXML Transitional）最初与更严格的变体 OOXML Strict 共存，后者消除了大部分有问题的遗留元素，但不是全部。 此外，Microsoft Office 始终使用 OOXML Transitional 作为默认格式，并将 OOXML Strict 降级到选项的底部（以防止使用它）。

实际效果是，数亿人每天使用的格式只有微软自己的软件才能正确实现，而其他软件实际上可以支持的更清晰的变体则没有被使用，现在甚至已经从某些版本中消失了。

从功能的角度来看，只有一个实现完全支持的标准是具有标准化证书的专有格式。

锁定，从个人到机构

对文档格式的依赖是锁定的主要机制，但不是唯一的机制。 我们在上一篇文章中详细讨论了依赖关系的分层，因此我们不会在这里重新讨论这个主题。 依赖程度取决于所涉及文档的重要性以及生成这些文档的组织的规模。

对于个人用户来说，布局改变的文档只会带来不便。 对于律师事务所来说，这可能意味着提交给法院的合同与客户文件中的版本不符。 对于医院来说，这可能意味着临床表格打印不正确。 对于政府部门来说，这可能意味着根据所使用的软件，文档的显示方式有所不同：一种无声的、无意的形式，对公共信息的访问不平等。

在公共行政层面，这种依赖性超出了运营效率的范围。 严格来说，以私营公司控制的格式归档官方文件的政府已将其机构记忆的保管权委托给了该公司。 今天，文档是可读的，因为微软继续支持该格式，但二十年后是否可以阅读它们将取决于公司的决定，其原因与公共利益无关。

这不是假设的风险：格式被淘汰，软件版本发生变化，一个版本的 Office 中提供的功能在另一个版本中可能表现不同，或者根本不起作用。

数字文档的历史中充斥着无法打开的文件，因为创建它们的软件已不存在或不再适用于现代系统。 专有格式将解释它们所需的知识集中在一个组织内，而该组织的商业利益可能随时与那些依赖于访问自己文档的人的利益产生分歧，从而加速了这种风险。

真正的主权需要什么

一份真正独立的文档——无论使用什么软件，在任何国家、任何系统上对任何用户都显示相同的文档——需要在其创建的每个阶段做出明智的选择。

在格式层面，它需要一个开放标准，例如开放文档格式（ODF），其规范由独立于任何单一供应商的机构发布、自由实施和管理。 ODF 是一项国际 ISO 标准，经过了合法的标准化流程，其规范可以由任何选择这样做的软件完全实施。 LibreOffice 是领先的开源办公套件，本身就使用 ODF。 这同样适用于任何其他有自尊的开源办公应用程序。

在字体方面，它需要开放字体，其设计是在许可下发布的，允许任何软件实现它们，并且任何用户可以无成本或无限制地安装它们。 开放字体存储库提供了各种没有专有依赖性的高质量选项。

在模板和工作流程方面，它需要将开放格式和开放字体指定为所有官方文档的默认设置的制度政策——不是作为审美偏好，而是作为治理要求，就像公共行政部门可能指定可访问性标准或数据保护要求一样。

在归档层面，这需要致力于专门为长期保存而设计的格式（例如用于永久归档的文档的 PDF/A），其规范是公开的，其可读性不取决于单个供应商的商业决策。

这些在技术上并不复杂，但需要有权这样做的人有意识地做出决定。

文件从来都不是无辜的

文档是可见的论证。 它也始终是一组不可见的依赖关系。

当一个机构发送一封采用专有字体、嵌入专有格式、由专有软件生成的信件时，它不是在传达信息，而是在工作流程中、在通讯员的期望中、在工作人员的记忆中以及在给收件人的隐含信息中永久存在一种依赖性，即这正是文档的工作方式，没有其他选择，书面通信的基础设施属于其他人，而且一直都是这样。

数字主权首先要认识到这是一种选择：文件格式是一种选择，页面上的字体是一种选择，软件也是一种选择。 与事实不同，选择可能会有所不同。

该文件仍然被认为是一张“看似”无辜的文件，实际上不再是一张纸——而且已经有一段时间了——而是一个由软件解释的可执行文件，因此不再是无辜的，而是在许多情况下成为阴险的锁定工具。
